description_long: This is a rustic, hand-carved wooden spoon with a rounded, shallow bowl and a long, slender handle. The handle features a decorative knob at the end, adding a touch of craftsmanship and character. The wood grain is visible throughout, with a slightly uneven texture and natural color variations, suggesting it is made from a single piece of wood. The surface appears matte and somewhat rough, with minor discoloration and signs of use. The spoon's bowl is broad and oval-shaped, suitable for scooping or serving. Overall, it has an aged, utilitarian appearance typical of traditional kitchen utensils.
description: A hand-carved wooden spoon with a rounded bowl and a long, slender handle featuring a decorative knob. The surface shows visible wood grain, minor wear, and a rustic, matte finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the spoon, highlighting the shallow bowl and decorative knob at the handle's end.
description_view_1: End-on view focusing on the knob and the thickness of the handle, with the bowl mostly obscured.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the broad, oval bowl and the straight handle, with wood grain and surface wear visible.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the curvature of the bowl and the tapering handle, with the knob partially visible.
attribute_orientation: bowl facing upward, handle extending to the left or right
attribute_contextual_placement: resting flat on a countertop or inside a utensil organizer
attribute_proximity_to_other_objects: often placed next to forks, knives, and other spoons; may be stored with similar wooden utensils
attribute_visible_wear_or_damage: minor discoloration and surface wear visible
